originalBehavior: The route opens in a coffeehouse framing scene with branching intro choices that establish tone before the main 2010 story begins.
localStatus: The coffeehouse menu, choice structure, and branch bookkeeping remain in the original runtime with local wording cleanup in the notes branches.
notes: The local port preserves the three-option opening menu and the branch flags it seeds for later dialogue.
Creator and provenance callouts in the framing branches were replaced with neutral story notes.
evidence: The current route still opens with the coffeehouse setup and menu-driven first choices.
Source structure keeps the original menu controller rather than replacing it with a custom shell.

originalBehavior: Players move through the Jack conversation and the multi-scene dinner sequence, with choices remembered and reflected by later dialogue.
localStatus: Jack, dinner, and parent-response branches stay in the original scene files with no local flattening of the conversation tree.
notes: Choice ordering, stored flags, and later callbacks still depend on the original runtime globals.
The local cleanup was intentionally limited to framing copy, not the central family-conversation branches.
evidence: Parity scope for this port centered on preserving the dinner-table branching path and consequence mapping.
The vendored scene files still hold the original branch logic and remembered-state checks.

originalBehavior: Dialogue options set state that later scenes recall, changing responses, callbacks, and which closure paths become available.
localStatus: Global choice state and conditional callbacks remain upstream-driven through the same scene-global object and queue system.
notes: The route still relies on remembered state rather than independent isolated scenes.
No local wrapper was added around the choice engine, so the original consequence mapping still drives progression.
evidence: The recheck plan explicitly called out consequence mapping as a parity target for this route.
Source still stores path decisions in the original runtime state object and reuses them across later scenes.

originalBehavior: The ending moves into an outro with multiple post-coming-out story variants, then reaches a final credits screen that supports replay by restarting the route.
localStatus: The lie, truth, and half-truth outro structure remains intact, while the visible end-screen copy was cleaned up for the local shell.
notes: The three-story closure loop and final scene transition remain in the original outro controller.
The public end screen keeps neutral replay guidance instead of external promo or creator links.
evidence: Current source still routes the ending to the same screen="credits" state as upstream.
Outro branches remain split across the original lie, truth, and half-truth sequences.

originalBehavior: The route loads audio, art, and dialogue assets into a lightweight shell with a loading bar before handing off to the conversation runtime.
localStatus: The loading screen, audio config, and scene shell remain upstream-driven, with local metadata and footer behavior layered on the document shell.
notes: The old static share bar was removed because it was not part of the core conversation runtime.
Canonical metadata and the shared footer are now handled at the outer shell only.
evidence: The route still opens with the original loading container and asset bootstrap sequence.
The local shell now resolves cleanly under /interactive-explanation/coming-out-simulator-2014/.
